name: How to Make Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown your food without burning the juices. Reduce heat to medium-high after initial searing on high heat.
2. Remove grease from the pan and set aside if used, or discard if no fat was added during cooking.
3. Add fresh butter or oil to the skillet and sauté chopped onion, shallots, garlic, or other aromatics over medium heat until slightly softened.
4. Deglaze the pan by adding 1/2–2/3 cup of liquid (stock, wine, fruit juice, water, cream, or a combination) to the skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Stir the mixture and raise the heat to high, scraping up all brown bits from the bottom.
6. Reduce the sauce until it has reduced by half and reaches a syrupy consistency.
7. Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, herbs, or other desired flavors.
